Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The plan diverges from the milestone brief's hard requirement to migrate every `usePanes()`/`PanesContext` consumer to selector-based store access and delete the old hooks: Step 2 and Step 3 explicitly keep `usePanes()` as a compatibility facade 'only where converting to selectors would create unnecessary churn', but the brief says 'Delete `PanesContext` + its provider + its hooks after migration' and 'Migrate every consumer of `usePanes()`'. With 28 current `usePanes()` call sites from `rg -l "usePanes\(" src`, leaving the facade in place would preserve the broad-subscription API the milestone is supposed to remove.
flags.evidence: Revised the plan to delete `usePanes()` entirely, use a complete `createContext` inventory regex, audit recomposed wrapper hooks alongside provider declarations, define one concrete fat-context counting rule, explicitly exclude local UI token contexts from migration scope, and use grep-driven checklists for all panes callers, tests, and provider-tree touch points.

flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The audit command quoted in the plan requirements is not safe to rely on literally in this repository. `rg 'createContext\(' src/ --type ts --type tsx -l` only finds the three Radix-style overlay contexts here because most app contexts are declared as `createContext<Type>(...)` or `React.createContext<Type>(...)`; a broader regex like `rg '(React\.)?createContext(<[^\n]+>)?\(' src ...` finds the actual context inventory including `PanesContext`, `ProjectContext`, `IncomingTasksContext`, `LightboxStateContext`, `ImageEditCanvasContext`, `VideoTravelSettingsProvider`, and `RealtimeProvider`. If the executor follows the plan's literal grep, the committed audit doc will omit most real providers and fail the 'listing each remaining createContext usage' requirement.
flags.evidence: Revised the plan to delete `usePanes()` entirely, use a complete `createContext` inventory regex, audit recomposed wrapper hooks alongside provider declarations, define one concrete fat-context counting rule, explicitly exclude local UI token contexts from migration scope, and use grep-driven checklists for all panes callers, tests, and provider-tree touch points.

flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The plan partially addresses the architectural audit requirement but scopes it too narrowly around raw `createContext` declarations. This codebase already has compatibility hooks that recombine split contexts into broad subscriptions, most visibly `useProject()` in `src/shared/contexts/ProjectContext.tsx`, which merges three contexts back together and currently has 43 call sites from `rg -l "useProject\(" src`. Since the brief calls out 'context-as-pubsub anti-patterns', the audit should explicitly review these recomposed hooks as well, not only the provider declarations.
flags.evidence: Revised the plan to delete `usePanes()` entirely, use a complete `createContext` inventory regex, audit recomposed wrapper hooks alongside provider declarations, define one concrete fat-context counting rule, explicitly exclude local UI token contexts from migration scope, and use grep-driven checklists for all panes callers, tests, and provider-tree touch points.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The compatibility-hook strategy is technically at odds with the stated performance goal. The current `usePanes()` implementation in `src/shared/contexts/PanesContext.tsx` recombines three context values into one object, and most consumers destructure only one or two fields from it, for example `useVideoGenerationModalController.ts` only reads `isShotsPaneLocked` and `setIsShotsPaneLocked`, `TimelineSection.tsx` only reads `isGenerationsPaneLocked`, and `GenerationsPane.tsx` only reads `effectiveGenerationsPaneHeight`. Replacing that with a store-backed `usePanes()` object plus `shallow` would reduce some churn, but any selector that returns the same wide object shape still keeps those call sites subscribed to multiple unrelated pane fields, so it does not fully deliver the 'opening one pane should not re-render every other pane' objective.
flags.evidence: Revised the plan to delete `usePanes()` entirely, use a complete `createContext` inventory regex, audit recomposed wrapper hooks alongside provider declarations, define one concrete fat-context counting rule, explicitly exclude local UI token contexts from migration scope, and use grep-driven checklists for all panes callers, tests, and provider-tree touch points.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The proposed fat-context guard is underspecified for this repository's actual context shapes. `LightboxStateContext` in `src/domains/media-lightbox/contexts/LightboxStateContext.tsx` exposes only four top-level properties (`core`, `media`, `variants`, `navigation`), but those nested groups contain a large mutable contract; `ImageEditCanvasContext` in `src/domains/media-lightbox/contexts/ImageEditCanvasContext.tsx` is the opposite case with a very large flat surface. A naive guard that only counts top-level fields in the provider type will miss broad grouped contexts like `LightboxStateContext`, while a naive flattening rule will disagree with the plan's current ambiguity notes. The plan needs one concrete counting rule before automation can be trusted.
flags.evidence: Revised the plan to delete `usePanes()` entirely, use a complete `createContext` inventory regex, audit recomposed wrapper hooks alongside provider declarations, define one concrete fat-context counting rule, explicitly exclude local UI token contexts from migration scope, and use grep-driven checklists for all panes callers, tests, and provider-tree touch points.

flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: The broader issue is not limited to `PanesContext`; the repository has the same 'split provider, recombined hook' pattern elsewhere. `ProjectContext.tsx` already split selection, CRUD, and identity into three contexts, but `useProject()` recombines them and is used in 43 places, many of which only need `selectedProjectId` or one mutation such as `updateProject`. That means the anti-pattern M4 is targeting still exists in another core app surface even if the raw provider count stays below the migration threshold.
flags.evidence: Revised the plan to delete `usePanes()` entirely, use a complete `createContext` inventory regex, audit recomposed wrapper hooks alongside provider declarations, define one concrete fat-context counting rule, explicitly exclude local UI token contexts from migration scope, and use grep-driven checklists for all panes callers, tests, and provider-tree touch points.

flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. If the audit only inspects the three contexts listed in Step 4 plus raw `useContext` call sites, it will miss at least two plausible over-threshold candidates.

flags.evidence: Revised the plan to delete `usePanes()` entirely, use a complete `createContext` inventory regex, audit recomposed wrapper hooks alongside provider declarations, define one concrete fat-context counting rule, explicitly exclude local UI token contexts from migration scope, and use grep-driven checklists for all panes callers, tests, and provider-tree touch points.

flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: `AppProviders.tsx` is a real integration point that the plan needs to treat as more than a one-line provider swap. `PanesProvider` is part of the top-level composed provider tree next to `ProjectProvider`, `RealtimeProvider`, `IncomingTasksProvider`, and `AgentChatProvider`, so replacing it with a bootstrap component must preserve mount order and any side effects that downstream hooks expect during initial render. The plan notes mounting a bootstrap here, which is necessary, but it understates how load-bearing this location is.

flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: The current `usePanes()` callers are predominantly narrow readers, not true consumers of a full panes object. Examples from grep: `useTaskNavigation.ts` only needs `setActiveTaskId` and `setIsTasksPaneOpen`, `useHomeNavigation.ts` only needs `setIsShotsPaneLocked`, `GlobalHeader/useGlobalHeaderController.ts` only needs `isGenerationsPaneLocked`, and `VideoShotDisplay.tsx` only needs `isGenerationsPaneLocked`. That caller profile argues for migrating to dedicated selector hooks rather than preserving a general `usePanes()` facade, because the broad API is the thing creating the subscription problem.
flags.evidence: Revised the plan to delete `usePanes()` entirely, use a complete `createContext` inventory regex, audit recomposed wrapper hooks alongside provider declarations, define one concrete fat-context counting rule, explicitly exclude local UI token contexts from migration scope, and use grep-driven checklists for all panes callers, tests, and provider-tree touch points.

flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: The current `useProject()` caller mix shows the same risk pattern the plan should account for during the wider audit. `useOnboardingFlow.ts`, `AppProviders.tsx`, `ShotsPage.tsx`, `TaskDetailsModal.tsx`, and many others only pull `selectedProjectId`, while a smaller set read CRUD actions or the `projects` list. Because `useProject()` merges three context values in one memoized object, those simple callers still subscribe to unrelated CRUD and identity changes. If M4 documents `ProjectContext` as a straight keep without discussing `useProject()`, the audit will miss a live compatibility-hook caller problem.

flags.evidence: Pinned down the remaining ambiguous threshold rule: wrapper-hook consumer counts now use only external production consumers, excluding same-file helper wiring, tests, and mocks. flags.concern: Context audit automation: the revised plan expands the required guard from the brief's fat-`createContext` check to semantic analysis of recomposed hooks like `useProject()` and `useVideoTravelSettings()`, which is broader than the approved requirement and adds avoidable policy/tooling scope to the milestone.
flags.evidence: The approved brief only requires a guard that flags new `createContext` usage with more than 10 fields, while revised step 6 requires automation for both `createContext` declarations and recomposed compatibility hooks.

flags.concern: Context audit inventory: Recurring debt: step 1's sample wrapper-hook grep still keys on `export function use...` and misses const-exported hooks such as `useProject` in `src/shared/contexts/ProjectContext.tsx`, leaving one of the largest recomposed access surfaces out of the supposedly exhaustive audit.
flags.evidence: Fixed the remaining audit-method defects by requiring imported local context value types to be expanded during field counting and by broadening the wrapper-hook inventory to catch both `export function` and `export const` hook exports such as `useProject`. flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Compared the revised plan with the existing store-test infrastructure in `src/shared/state/selectionStore.ts` and related test files. Because this repo uses singleton Zustand stores plus explicit `__reset...ForTests()` helpers to avoid cross-test contamination, deleting `PanesProvider` in favor of a panes store also needs an explicit reset helper and test-harness migration path; the plan currently mentions general test updates but does not call out that supporting infrastructure, which leaves a real risk of order-dependent pane-state leakage in suites that stop mocking the old context module.
flags.evidence: Narrowed the permanent automation back to the brief’s actual requirement: guarding new fat `createContext(...)` declarations only. flags.concern: Context audit counting: the revised 'same-file one level deep' rule undercounts imported context value types, so obviously fat contexts can be misclassified as thin. `ShotSettingsContext.provider.tsx` imports `ShotSettingsContextValue` from `ShotSettingsContext.types.ts`; following the plan literally would count that declaration as opaque even though the actual value shape is far above the >10-field threshold and has 6 production consumers.
